summarization.a: The figure presents a DFT-derived energetic landscape for HF adsorption on the θ-Al₂O₃ ( 2̄01 ) surface, showing five representative configurations (M⁰, M₁ₐ, M₁ʙ, M₁c, M₁ᴅ). The clean surface (M⁰) is taken as the reference state, while molecular HF adsorption (M₁ₐ) is weakly stabilizing. Dissociative adsorption configurations are substantially more stable, with progressive hydrogen migration leading to lower energies. Among the identified minima, M₁ᴅ—where the dissociated hydrogen atom relocates to an oxygen atom in an adjacent row—is the most energetically favorable configuration.

data_extraction.a:
| State   | Description                                      | Binding energy (eV) |
|---------|--------------------------------------------------|---------------------|
| M⁰      | Clean θ-Al₂O₃ (¯201) surface                     | 0.00 (reference)    |
| M₁ₐ     | Molecular (undissociated) HF adsorption          | –0.44               |
| M₁ʙ     | Dissociative adsorption (H–O, F–Al)              | –1.74               |
| M₁c     | Dissociated H hopped to adjacent O (same row)    | –1.31               |
| M₁ᴅ     | Dissociated H hopped to O in adjacent row        | –2.11               |

a.question: Which HF adsorption configuration is most energetically stable on the θ-Al₂O₃ (¯201) surface according to this figure?
a.answer: The most energetically stable configuration shown is M₁ᴅ, with a binding energy of –2.11 eV. In this state, HF has dissociated and the hydrogen atom has relocated to an oxygen site in an adjacent surface row. The lower energy relative to other configurations indicates that this dissociative arrangement is thermodynamically favored among the states considered.

a.question: What is the energetic ordering of the adsorption states from weakest to strongest binding?
a.answer:
• M₁ₐ (molecular adsorption): –0.44 eV  
, • M₁c (dissociative, same-row H hop): –1.31 eV  
, • M₁ʙ (dissociative adsorption): –1.74 eV  
, • M₁ᴅ (dissociative, adjacent-row H hop): –2.11 eV

a.question: How much more stable is the most favorable dissociative adsorption state compared to molecular adsorption?
a.answer: The most favorable dissociative state (M₁ᴅ) is more stable than molecular adsorption (M₁ₐ) by approximately 1.67 eV.

a.question: Do the binding energies shown suggest that HF prefers dissociative adsorption over molecular adsorption on θ-Al₂O₃?
